summary: We are in a high-stakes sprint to launch the China Going-Global report and Intelligent China program by September, while simultaneously optimizing SEO and campaign performance for Asia Star and Asia Start.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the joint CKGSB-IE report 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', targeting a mid-September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore. The team is translating the Chinese report, drafting op-eds from both schools' leadership, and meeting with Tech in Asia this week to finalize the session theme. Urgent this week: finalize the report structure and promotion one-pager, confirm op-ed contributions, and start translating selected sections. Concurrently, the Asia Start campaign is live with two parallel routes (AI vs. traditional) but is currently in observation mode due to a targeting issue that needs diagnosis and fix by July 2. The Intelligent China program, launching in December, is being positioned as a deeper dive than Asia Start; website and SCM campaign planning are underway. On the SEO front, the team is prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China over US partnerships, with Google Analytics conversion tracking setup and search visibility lists due by June 30. Finally, social media agency selection is being expedited, with Media Minds as top candidate, and several content pieces (Shun Tian Shu press release, homepage copy, Ouyang Hui article) are being finalized for review and publication this week.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy for CKGSB programs, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China over US partnerships, and reviews Google Analytics conversion tracking setup.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed website menu feedback, brochure updates, CES quotes, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, social media agency scouting, and IE report translation. Decided to consolidate Shun Tian Shu press release and expedite agency selection.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds and case studies, aiming for launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and a new Intelligent China program launching in December, targeting foreigners with in-depth modules across Beijing, Shanghai, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: China-Europe Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on China-Europe relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; US partnerships (Stanford, Columbia, Berkeley) are secondary, with Berkeley as possible third priority.
Consolidate Shun Tian Shu press release into one version, not two.
Expedite social media agency selection; consider Media Minds as top candidate.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
